outlierOnlySources: A-Tech/Newegg low cluster vs Provantage/CDW high cluster

noDirectSampleSources: Provantage exact 30.72 low-band anchor no longer reproducible

notes: DDR5-5600 32GB remains the longest-running structural split: public prices exist every day, but they still do not converge into a trustworthy single market band.

PCIe4 TLC core slots stayed stable for 7-14 days, but 30.72TB still needs protection against false low-channel repricing because new low clusters keep surfacing outside the established CDW/SHI/DigiKey band.

PCIe5 TLC recovered nominal 5/5 coverage yesterday, but the real unresolved issue is 30.72TB anchor decay: the prior Kioxia low-band stopped repeating cleanly, while new low and high clusters diverged further.

PCIe4 QLC had heavy sample churn over the last week; today the category no longer depends on CDW, but the clean public band now differs by capacity, so the category is improving in breadth rather than in uniformity.

keyChangesVsYesterday: DDR5-5600 32GB gained a fresh low-side public source from A-Tech Memory at $950.39, but high-side Provantage/CDW pricing remains intact, so the slot stays outlier_only rather than recovering a representative value.

PCIe5 TLC 30.72TB widened again: the old Provantage Kioxia low-band anchor was not reproducible today, AAAWave showed $4,198, while Howard Computers and A5 IT kept Micron high-band pricing around $14.6k-$15.0k.

PCIe4 QLC decoupled further from CDW: CDW reverted 15.36/30.72/61.44TB pages to product_price=0, but Newegg/Connection/Mouser provided fresh public prices; 15.36TB repriced lower while 30.72TB repriced higher.

DDR5-6400 remained broadly flat: 64GB exact Provantage and 128GB exact ComputerDealsDirect anchors stayed intact, while 32GB only added a search-grounded Tech-America low-side hint instead of a clean new exact page.

DDR5-6400.reason: 64GB and 128GB anchors remain stable enough to buy against, but 32GB today leaned more on search-grounded/public-low-side evidence than on a fresh exact anchor, so full-size commitment is still unnecessary.

DDR5-5600.reason: 32GB still has a cleanly observable two-layer market and 64GB/128GB are visible but not cheap; coverage exists, yet the category is not offering an attractive, unified buy signal.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: The core 3.84/7.68/15.36TB band remains the most stable in the whole sheet and 30.72TB still has a public, repeatable distribution band despite low-channel outliers.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: 1.92TB and some lower-capacity PM1743 public prices remain usable, but 30.72TB now clearly requires family-by-family and channel-by-channel buying rather than a single market quote.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: The category gained broader public confirmation outside CDW, but the direction is mixed by capacity: 15.36TB eased, 30.72TB rose, 61.44TB stayed high-side, and 122.88TB remained in the low-$40k cluster.

gaps.note: Today again showed a persistent low cluster (A-Tech / Newegg) and a persistent high cluster (Provantage / CDW), so the slot still cannot support a single trustworthy representative value.
Multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public price today.
The slot remains dependent on older or quote-first clues; today did not recover a fresh clean public quote.
The previously preferred lower-band anchor did not repeat cleanly today; the new low-side AAAWave signal is too isolated and conflicts sharply with same-day premium Micron public prices.

validationNotes: Provantage still works well for direct DDR5 HTML price extraction, but only for some exact parts; the 6400 32GB slot lost the easy exact public anchor today.
Memory.net is important as a coverage source, but today it stayed firmly in spec_or_rfq rather than public-price territory.
CDW continues to oscillate between real public pricing and product_price=0 pages; zero-price pages were treated as coverage deterioration rather than market repricing.
AAAWave is a real public source for KCMYXRUG30T7 today, but its 30.72TB price is too isolated versus the same-day premium family band to overwrite the representative value alone.
Mouser and Connection materially improved QLC breadth today, especially for 15.36/30.72/61.44TB, even though direct page access and stock posture remain inconsistent.
